cards.title: Group Axioms & Properties
cards.description: The fundamental properties that define mathematical groups and their structures. This card covers key aspects including A group \( G \) is a set with a binary operation \( \cdot \) satisfying four axioms: closure, associativity, identity, and inverses, A group is abelian if \( a \cdot b = b \cdot a \) for all \( a, b \in G \), otherwise it is non-abelian, Examples include \( (\mathbb{Z}, +) \), the symmetric group \( S_n \), and the general linear group \( GL(n, \mathbb{R}) \), providing practical knowledge applicable to mathematics, physics equations, and quantitative analysis.
bulletpoints: A group \( G \) is a set with a binary operation \( \cdot \) satisfying four axioms: closure, associativity, identity, and inverses
A group is abelian if \( a \cdot b = b \cdot a \) for all \( a, b \in G \), otherwise it is non-abelian
Examples include \( (\mathbb{Z}, +) \), the symmetric group \( S_n \), and the general linear group \( GL(n, \mathbb{R}) \)

Advanced practitioners demonstrate competency through equation derivation, applying theoretical knowledge to novel situations and adapting standard procedures to meet the specific demands of each unique operational context encountered

cards.title: Cyclic Groups & Generators
cards.description: Understanding groups generated by a single element. bulletpoints: A group \( G \) is cyclic if there exists an element \( g \in G \) such that \( G = \{ g^n \mid n \in \mathbb{Z} \} \)

cards.title: Permutation Groups & Symmetric Groups

This card covers key aspects including The symmetric group \( S_n \) consists of all permutations of \( n \) elements under composition, Every permutation can be written as a product of disjoint cycles, The alternating group \( A_n \) consists of all even permutations, forming a normal subgroup of \( S_n \), providing practical knowledge applicable to mathematics, physics equations, and quantitative analysis.

cards.title: Dihedral Groups & Rotational Symmetry

bulletpoints: The dihedral group \( D_n \) consists of \( 2n \) elements: \( n \) rotations and \( n \) reflections

cards.title: Normal Subgroups & Quotient Groups
cards.description: Understanding subgroup structure and factorization in groups. This card covers key aspects including A subgroup \( N \) of \( G \) is normal if \( gNg^{-1} = N \) for all \( g \in G \), The quotient group \( G/N \) consists of left cosets of \( N \), forming a new group, Examples include \( \mathbb{Z}/n\mathbb{Z} \), fundamental in modular arithmetic and homomorphisms, providing practical knowledge applicable to mathematics, physics equations, and quantitative analysis.
bulletpoints: A subgroup \( N \) of \( G \) is normal if \( gNg^{-1} = N \) for all \( g \in G \)
The quotient group \( G/N \) consists of left cosets of \( N \), forming a new group

cards.title: Common Factoring Methods
cards.description: Understanding basic factoring techniques to simplify polynomials. This card covers key aspects including Factor out the greatest common factor (GCF): \[ ax^n + bx^{n-1} = x^{n-1} (ax + b) \], Recognize special binomial patterns: \[ a^2 - b^2 = (a - b)(a + b) \] (difference of squares), Factor sum and difference of cubes: \[ a^3 - b^3 = (a - b)(a^2 + ab + b^2) \], \[ a^3 + b^3 = (a + b)(a^2 - ab + b^2) \], providing practical knowledge applicable to mathematics, physics equations, and quantitative analysis.
bulletpoints: Factor out the greatest common factor (GCF): \[ ax^n + bx^{n-1} = x^{n-1} (ax + b) \]

Factor sum and difference of cubes: \[ a^3 - b^3 = (a - b)(a^2 + ab + b^2) \], \[ a^3 + b^3 = (a + b)(a^2 - ab + b^2) \]

cards.title: Finding Roots & Graphing Polynomials
cards.description: Understanding how to locate the zeros of polynomial functions and analyze their graphs. This card covers key aspects including Find polynomial roots by factoring completely: \[ (x - 2)(x + 3) = 0 \Rightarrow x = 2, -3 \], Apply the Rational Root Theorem: Possible rational roots of \( ax^n + bx^{n-1} + ... + c \) are \( \pm \frac{p}{q} \), where \( p \) and \( q \) are factors of the constant term and leading coefficient., Analyze end behavior using the degree and leading coefficient:, providing practical knowledge applicable to mathematics, physics equations, and quantitative analysis.
bulletpoints: Find polynomial roots by factoring completely: \[ (x - 2)(x + 3) = 0 \Rightarrow x = 2, -3 \]

Apply the Rational Root Theorem: Possible rational roots of \( ax^n + bx^{n-1} + ... + c \) are \( \pm \frac{p}{q} \), where \( p \) and \( q \) are factors of the constant term and leading coefficient.

Analyze end behavior using the degree and leading coefficient: — this concept is critical for effective finding roots & graphing polynomials because practitioners must understand how it integrates with related operational procedures and real-world application contexts
- If \( n \) is even and \( a_n > 0 \), both ends point up.
- If \( n \) is even and \( a_n < 0 \), both ends point down.
- If \( n \) is odd and \( a_n > 0 \), the function rises to the right and falls to the left.
- If \( n \) is odd and \( a_n < 0 \), the function falls to the right and rises to the left.

bulletpoints: A linear inequality has the form \( ax + b > c \) — this concept is critical for effective solving and graphing inequalities because practitioners must understand how it integrates with related operational procedures and real-world application contexts

Graph solutions on a number line, using open/closed circles for strict/non-strict inequalities — this concept is critical for effective solving and graphing inequalities because practitioners must understand how it integrates with related operational procedures and real-world application contexts

This card covers key aspects including Define a limit using epsilon-delta notation: \[ \lim_{x \to a} f(x) = L \iff \forall \epsilon > 0, \exists \delta > 0, \text{ such that } |x - a| < \delta \Rightarrow |f(x) - L| < \epsilon \], Evaluate limits using direct substitution, factoring, and L'Hôpital’s Rule: \[ \lim_{x \to 0} \frac{\sin x}{x} = 1 \], Identify continuous and discontinuous functions by checking if: \[ \lim_{x \to a} f(x) = f(a) \], providing practical knowledge applicable to mathematics, physics equations, and quantitative analysis.
bulletpoints: Define a limit using epsilon-delta notation: \[ \lim_{x \to a} f(x) = L \iff \forall \epsilon > 0, \exists \delta > 0, \text{ such that } |x - a| < \delta \Rightarrow |f(x) - L| < \epsilon \]
Evaluate limits using direct substitution, factoring, and L'Hôpital’s Rule: \[ \lim_{x \to 0} \frac{\sin x}{x} = 1 \]
Identify continuous and discontinuous functions by checking if: \[ \lim_{x \to a} f(x) = f(a) \]

Advanced practitioners demonstrate competency through equation derivation, applying theoretical knowledge to novel situations and adapting standard procedures to meet the specific demands of each unique operational context encountered

cards.title: Basic Derivative Rules
cards.description: Learning fundamental differentiation rules to compute rates of change. This card covers key aspects including Power Rule: \[ \frac{d}{dx} x^n = n x^{n-1} \], Product Rule: \[ \frac{d}{dx} [uv] = u' v + u v' \], Quotient Rule: \[ \frac{d}{dx} \left( \frac{u}{v} \right) = \frac{u' v - u v'}{v^2} \], providing practical knowledge applicable to mathematics, physics equations, and quantitative analysis.
bulletpoints: Power Rule: \[ \frac{d}{dx} x^n = n x^{n-1} \] — this concept is critical for effective basic derivative rules because practitioners must understand how it integrates with related operational procedures and real-world application contexts

Product Rule: \[ \frac{d}{dx} [uv] = u' v + u v' \] — this concept is critical for effective basic derivative rules because practitioners must understand how it integrates with related operational procedures and real-world application contexts

Chain Rule: \[ \frac{d}{dx} f(g(x)) = f'(g(x)) g'(x) \] — this concept is critical for effective basic derivative rules because practitioners must understand how it integrates with related operational procedures and real-world application contexts
Differentiate exponential and trigonometric functions: \[ \frac{d}{dx} e^x = e^x, \quad \frac{d}{dx} \sin x = \cos x \]

cards.description: Understanding how convolutional layers extract spatial features from images. cards.title: Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) & Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM)
cards.description: Exploring networks designed for sequential data processing. cards.title: McCulloch-Pitts Neuron Model
cards.description: A simple mathematical model representing binary threshold-based neural activation. This card covers key aspects including The McCulloch-Pitts neuron defines activation as: \[ y = \begin{cases} 1, & \text{if } \sum_i w_i x_i + b > \text{threshold} \\ 0, & \text{otherwise} \end{cases} \], This model is used to represent the simplest form of neural computation, where a neuron fires if the weighted sum of inputs exceeds a threshold., This equation forms the basis of perceptron learning and is useful in binary classification tasks, such as logic gates, pattern recognition, and rule-based AI systems., providing practical knowledge applicable to mathematics, physics equations, and quantitative analysis.

cards.title: Perceptron Learning Rule
cards.description: A fundamental algorithm for training single-layer perceptrons by adjusting weights based on error. This card covers key aspects including The weight update rule for a perceptron is given by: \[ \Delta w_i = \eta (d - y) x_i \], Here, \( \eta \) is the learning rate, \( d \) is the desired output, and \( y \) is the actual output., This rule adjusts the weights when an error occurs, guiding the perceptron towards better classification in linearly separable problems, such as handwritten digit recognition and medical diagnosis models., providing practical knowledge applicable to mathematics, physics equations, and quantitative analysis.
bulletpoints: The weight update rule for a perceptron is given by: \[ \Delta w_i = \eta (d - y) x_i \]

This rule adjusts the weights when an error occurs, guiding the perceptron towards better classification in linearly separable problems, such as handwritten digit recognition and medical diagnosis models.

cards.title: Backpropagation Algorithm
cards.description: A key optimization technique used to train multi-layer neural networks by minimizing error. This card covers key aspects including The backpropagation algorithm updates the weight gradients using: \[ \delta^L = \nabla_a C \odot \sigma'(z^L) \], This equation calculates the error at layer \( L \), propagating it backward to adjust weights using gradient descent., Backpropagation allows deep neural networks to learn complex patterns by minimizing the loss function in tasks like image recognition, speech processing, and financial forecasting., providing practical knowledge applicable to mathematics, physics equations, and quantitative analysis.
bulletpoints: The backpropagation algorithm updates the weight gradients using: \[ \delta^L = \nabla_a C \odot \sigma'(z^L) \]

This card covers key aspects including The softmax function is defined as: \[ P(y_i) = \frac{e^{z_i}}{\sum_{j} e^{z_j}} \], This function ensures that outputs sum to 1, making it ideal for multi-class classification tasks., Softmax is widely used in deep learning models for NLP, object detection, and reinforcement learning., providing practical knowledge applicable to mathematics, physics equations, and quantitative analysis.

cards.title: Batch Normalization
cards.description: A technique that stabilizes and speeds up neural network training by normalizing activations. This card covers key aspects including Batch normalization is defined as: \[ x' = \frac{x - \mu}{\sqrt{\sigma^2 + \epsilon}} \], This equation ensures that activations have zero mean and unit variance, preventing internal covariate shift., Batch normalization improves convergence rates, regularization, and generalization in deep networks., providing practical knowledge applicable to mathematics, physics equations, and quantitative analysis.
bulletpoints: Batch normalization is defined as: \[ x' = \frac{x - \mu}{\sqrt{\sigma^2 + \epsilon}} \] — this concept is critical for effective batch normalization because practitioners must understand how it integrates with related operational procedures and real-world application contexts

cards.title: Hadamard Product: Element-Wise Multiplication
cards.description: Understanding the fundamental mathematical operation that defines \( \odot \). This card covers key aspects including The Hadamard product is defined as: \[ \mathbf{A} \odot \mathbf{B} = \begin{bmatrix} a_1 b_1 \\ a_2 b_2 \\ \vdots \\ a_n b_n \end{bmatrix} \], Unlike matrix multiplication, this operation multiplies corresponding elements of two matrices of the same shape., Hadamard products are frequently used in gradient updates, activation function derivatives, and recurrent neural networks., providing practical knowledge applicable to mathematics, physics equations, and quantitative analysis.
bulletpoints: The Hadamard product is defined as: \[ \mathbf{A} \odot \mathbf{B} = \begin{bmatrix} a_1 b_1 \\ a_2 b_2 \\ \vdots \\ a_n b_n \end{bmatrix} \]

Hadamard products are frequently used in gradient updates, activation function derivatives, and recurrent neural networks.

cards.title: Backpropagation Delta Rule
cards.description: A core equation in training neural networks that uses \( \odot \) to compute gradients efficiently. This card covers key aspects including The delta rule in backpropagation is given by: \[ \delta^L = \nabla_a C \odot \sigma'(z^L) \], Here, \( \nabla_a C \) represents the gradient of the cost function with respect to activations, and \( \sigma'(z^L) \) is the derivative of the activation function., This Hadamard product ensures that errors are scaled correctly per neuron, helping to adjust weights efficiently., providing practical knowledge applicable to mathematics, physics equations, and quantitative analysis.
bulletpoints: The delta rule in backpropagation is given by: \[ \delta^L = \nabla_a C \odot \sigma'(z^L) \]
Here, \( \nabla_a C \) represents the gradient of the cost function with respect to activations, and \( \sigma'(z^L) \) is the derivative of the activation function.
This Hadamard product ensures that errors are scaled correctly per neuron, helping to adjust weights efficiently.

cards.title: LSTM Forget Gate Computation
cards.description: Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks rely on \( \odot \) to regulate information flow in memory cells. This card covers key aspects including The forget gate in LSTM is defined as: \[ c_t = f_t \odot c_{t-1} + i_t \odot g_t \], Here, \( f_t \) determines how much past information is retained, while \( i_t \odot g_t \) represents new memory input., The Hadamard product enables element-wise gating, allowing each unit to selectively retain or forget information., providing practical knowledge applicable to mathematics, physics equations, and quantitative analysis.
bulletpoints: The forget gate in LSTM is defined as: \[ c_t = f_t \odot c_{t-1} + i_t \odot g_t \]
Here, \( f_t \) determines how much past information is retained, while \( i_t \odot g_t \) represents new memory input.
The Hadamard product enables element-wise gating, allowing each unit to selectively retain or forget information.

cards.title: LSTM Output Gate Computation
cards.description: LSTM output gating determines which parts of the memory cell are used for the next hidden state. This card covers key aspects including The output gate is computed as: \[ h_t = o_t \odot \tanh(c_t) \], Here, \( o_t \) is the output gate activation and \( \tanh(c_t) \) represents the transformed memory cell state., Hadamard product ensures that only relevant memory is passed to the next state, improving sequence prediction performance., providing practical knowledge applicable to mathematics, physics equations, and quantitative analysis.

cards.title: Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) State Update

bulletpoints: The GRU hidden state update is given by: \[ h_t = (1 - z_t) \odot h_{t-1} + z_t \odot \tilde{h}_t \]

cards.title: Element-Wise Weight Decay in Optimization
cards.description: Hadamard product is used to apply weight decay regularization per element. This card covers key aspects including The weight decay update rule is: \[ w_i' = w_i - \eta \left( \frac{\partial C}{\partial w_i} \odot \lambda w_i \right) \], Here, \( \lambda \) represents the regularization coefficient that penalizes large weights., Using element-wise multiplication prevents uniform shrinkage, allowing finer control over optimization., providing practical knowledge applicable to mathematics, physics equations, and quantitative analysis.
bulletpoints: The weight decay update rule is: \[ w_i' = w_i - \eta \left( \frac{\partial C}{\partial w_i} \odot \lambda w_i \right) \]

Here, \( \lambda \) represents the regularization coefficient that penalizes large weights. — this concept is critical for effective element-wise weight decay in optimization because practitioners must understand how it integrates with related operational procedures and real-world application contexts

Using element-wise multiplication prevents uniform shrinkage, allowing finer control over optimization. — this concept is critical for effective element-wise weight decay in optimization because practitioners must understand how it integrates with related operational procedures and real-world application contexts

equations_symbolic_logic_and_proofs_form.name: Formal Proof Methods
equations_symbolic_logic_and_proofs_form.description: Mastering the different proof techniques used in mathematics and logic.

cards.title: Direct Proofs & Logical Deduction
cards.description: Learning how to construct step-by-step logical arguments. This card covers key aspects including A direct proof shows that \( P \Rightarrow Q \) by assuming \( P \) and deriving \( Q \), Example: Prove that if \( n \) is even, then \( n^2 \) is even: \[ n = 2k \Rightarrow n^2 = 4k^2 = 2(2k^2) \], Apply direct proofs in number theory, inequalities, and algebra., providing practical knowledge applicable to mathematics, physics equations, and quantitative analysis.
bulletpoints: A direct proof shows that \( P \Rightarrow Q \) by assuming \( P \) and deriving \( Q \)

cards.title: Predicate Logic & Logical Quantifiers
cards.description:
Exploring the role of quantifiers in mathematical statements. This card covers key aspects including Understand the universal quantifier: \[  orall x \in S, P(x) \] means \( P(x) \) is true for all elements \( x \) in \( S \), Understand the existential quantifier: \[ \exists x \in S, P(x) \] means there exists at least one \( x \) in \( S \) for which \( P(x) \) is true., Learn how to negate quantified statements: \[ 
eg ( orall x, P(x)) = \exists x, 
eg P(x) \], \[ 
eg (\exists x, P(x)) =  orall x, 
eg P(x) \], providing practical knowledge applicable to mathematics, physics equations, and quantitative analysis.

cards.title: Relativistic Time Dilation & Gravity Wells
cards.description: Understanding how velocity and gravity alter the flow of time. This card covers key aspects including Study Einstein’s special relativity time dilation equation: \[ \Delta t' = \frac{\Delta t}{\sqrt{1 - \frac{v^2}{c^2}}} \], where \( v \) is velocity and \( c \) is the speed of light., Analyze the gravitational time dilation effect from general relativity: \[ \Delta t' = \Delta t \sqrt{1 - \frac{2GM}{rc^2}} \], where \( M \) is mass and \( r \) is radial distance., Explore experimental evidence of time dilation using atomic clocks on satellites and high-speed jets., providing practical knowledge applicable to mathematics, physics equations, and quantitative analysis.
